admin.noToken: To manage events, use the moderator link you received by email.
admin.noEvents: No events found. Create your first event.
admin.noRegistrations: No participants registered yet. Share the event link!
admin.startEventSuccess: Event started! Waiting participants will be redirected automatically.
admin.jvbWarmupWarning: Video servers are starting up (~2 minutes). Participants will see a notice in the waiting room.
admin.dateChangedNotification: The date has been changed. Registered participants will be notified via email.
admin.createEventExplanation: Create your event and you will receive a link to manage it.
admin.noAccountNeeded: No account needed — the moderator link is your access to manage the event.

form.expectedParticipantsHint: Just an estimate for infrastructure sizing. This is not a hard cap: registrations beyond this number are accepted and the infra scales automatically.
form.expectedSenderRatioHint: Percentage of attendees with mic or webcam on. Classic webinar 10-20%, panel 50-100%. Leave empty to inherit the site default.

jvbPreview.title: Estimated JVB capacity
jvbPreview.videoDisabled: Video disabled: all attendees count as receivers.
jvbPreview.warnInherited: With {participants} attendees and the inherited ratio ({defaultPct}%), the estimate relies on the site default. Large events should set an explicit value (webinar 5-20%, meeting 30%, workshop 60%).
jvbPreview.warnCeiling: Estimate at the configured max ({max} pods). If real attendance or senders exceed the estimate, bridges may saturate. Consider raising the cap or lowering maxParticipants.

form.gracePeriodHint: How many minutes past the scheduled end to keep the call open. Shown as a non-intrusive banner to attendees during overtime.
form.agendaEnabledDesc: A checklist of topics to cover that moderators tick off during the event. Feeds the automatic summary. Optional and can also be enabled during the event.
form.whiteboardEnabledDesc: Enables the native collaborative whiteboard (Excalidraw): moderators can open it from the toolbar during the event. Always on for instant calls.
form.autoStartRecordingDesc: When the event starts, recording begins on its own (the moderator is not prompted). Late joiners immediately find a catch-up player available.
form.aiSectionDesc: After the event ends, the recording can be processed by an in-cluster AI pipeline to produce transcript, summary and — if requested — translations and dubbing. Everything runs on the Department's infrastructure, no data leaves to third parties.
form.aiTranscriptEnabledDesc: Speech recognition with speaker identification. Required to enable summary, translations and dubbing.
form.aiSummaryEnabledDesc: Meeting summary, decisions taken, action items and split into chapters with timestamps.
form.aiTranslationEnabledDesc: Transcript and summary translated into the languages set below.
form.aiTargetLocalesDesc: ISO language codes (e.g. en, fr, de) separated by commas. Leave empty to use the site-wide languages.
form.aiTargetLocalesRequired: Set at least one translation language, or turn off automatic translation.
form.aiDubbingEnabledDesc: Dubbed audio in the target languages, generated with pre-trained synthetic voices. Does not clone participants' voices.
form.multitrackRecordingEnabledDesc: Records a separate audio track per participant so the transcript attributes who said what with certainty (no more "Speaker 1/2"). It processes isolated single-speaker audio (sensitive personal data): tracks are temporary and deleted right after transcription. Requires recording consent.
form.retainParticipantTracksDesc: Keeps the individual audio tracks beyond transcription, for the downloadable archive and per-speaker playback. WARNING: this extends retention of sensitive personal data (isolated voice) until the event's retention deadline. Enable only if needed and with adequate consent/notice.
form.expectedSpeakersDesc: When known (e.g. 3 speakers), setting this improves speaker identification quality. Leave empty to let the pipeline decide.
form.dataRetentionHint: After this period, participant registrations, Q&A questions and any video recordings will be automatically deleted.
form.waitingRoomAudioUrlHint: Enter the URL of an MP3 file for waiting room music. Leave empty to use the default audio.
form.permissionsOnDesc: Participants can activate on their own
form.permissionsOffDesc: Only moderators can activate. Participants must raise their hand.
form.permissionsNote: Permission changes apply to new participants.
form.privacyPolicyTemplateEmpty: This template has no text yet. Edit it from Settings › GDPR templates.
form.privacyPolicyTemplateHint: Templates are managed under Settings › GDPR templates. ★ marks the default.
form.privacyPolicyTextHint: Write the privacy policy text. It will be shown inline in the registration form.
form.retentionPreviewIntro: After {days} days from the end of the event, the following will be automatically deleted:
form.retentionPreviewKept: Aggregate statistics and event metadata are retained.
form.templateApplied: Template applied: {name}. You can modify all settings.

links.publicPageHint: Share this URL to collect new registrations.
links.guestJoinHint: Quick link to invite a last-minute attendee: they join as a guest while the event is live, without filling in the registration form.
links.moderatorLinkHint: Share this link only with moderators. Anyone with this link can manage the event.
links.liveRoomHint: Direct link to enter the live room as moderator.

recordingUpload.hint: Formats: MP4, WebM, MOV, M4V. Max 5 GB. Upload goes straight to Azure Blob storage.

coModerators.introHint: Each person has their own display name in the room and a token that can be revoked independently. Speakers get mic, camera and screen share but can't moderate (kick, mute-all, recording).
coModerators.name: Name visible in room
coModerators.roleModeratorHint: Full moderation: record, mute everyone, remove participants.
coModerators.roleSpeakerHint: Can speak, show video and share screen. Cannot moderate the room.
coModerators.confirmRevoke: Revoke this person's access? The link will stop working immediately.
coModerators.empty: No extra access granted. The primary moderator link is the only privileged entry.
coModerators.activeCount: {count, plural, =0 {No active access} =1 {1 active access} other {# active accesses}}

settings.title: Site settings
settings.subtitle: Configure the platform appearance and behavior for PA reuse

sections.languagesDescription: Enable or disable the EU languages exposed to users, pick the default locale and customise translation strings.
sections.gdprTemplatesDescription: Create reusable privacy notices that can be linked to any event, and pick the default one for new events.
sections.emailTemplatesDescription: Customise subject, headings and copy for the confirmation and reminder emails. Leave empty to keep the built-in defaults.
sections.tagsDescription: Create and edit the tags used to filter events

postprod.prerequisitesNote: Post-production works on the full recording once the event has ended. The dedicated GPU pool wakes up when the queue receives its first job and shuts down when the queue empties, to keep costs down. Before enabling the pipeline make sure the cluster has the GPU pool configured, the NVIDIA operator installed, the HuggingFace credentials available in the namespace and the model weights pre-loaded on the dedicated volume. The operational checklist lives in docs/POSTPROD.md.
postprod.pipelineEnabledHelp: Master switch. When disabled, no transcript, summary, translation or dubbing is produced for new events, even if individual events have post-production turned on. Artifacts already generated stop being exposed to the public until you switch the pipeline back on. Use it to pause everything without redeploying the application.
postprod.timingNote: The pipeline never touches the live event. Everything runs after the event ends, once the recording is archived: subtitles and summaries are never available in real time. The choice prioritises transcription quality and contains GPU consumption compared to live captioning.
postprod.providersIntro: The engines used for transcription, summarisation and dubbing are declared at deployment time. Adding a new one is an infrastructure task (deploy the engine in cluster, register it in the supported provider list, update the schema). The dropdowns below show the engines currently available on this instance.
postprod.asrProviderHelp: Generates the event transcript with per-minute timing and speaker separation. We use WhisperX large-v3 alongside pyannote.audio for diarization. Both models run in cluster on the weights stored on the dedicated volume.
postprod.llmProviderHelp: Produces the meeting summary, identifies the topics discussed and translates transcript and summary into the target languages. Model Mistral-Small-3.2-24B (European vendor, Apache 2.0 license) served via vLLM in cluster.
postprod.ttsEngineHelp: Synthesises audio in the chosen languages using pre-trained public voices. When the event has more than one speaker, each speaker gets a different voice from the available pool to help listeners follow the conversation: the assignment is arbitrary and never reproduces the real voice of the participants. Dubbing is only generated when the organiser requests it for the specific event.
postprod.voiceCloningNote: The system uses generic synthetic voices only (Piper TTS, public models under MIT license). It does not analyse, store or reproduce the voice of participants: each speaker is represented by a voice from the pool, chosen deterministically based on speaking time, with no link to their vocal identity. The assigned voice changes with the speaker composition of each event, so the same speaker can be dubbed with different voices in different events. This rules out the processing of biometric data covered by article 9 of the GDPR.
postprod.defaultTargetLocalesHelp: ISO-639-1 codes separated by commas (e.g. `en,fr,de`). They apply to events that do not specify their own translation languages. The source language of the event is always excluded automatically.
postprod.maxConcurrentJobsHelp: Maximum number of jobs the pipeline runs in parallel on the GPU pool. Higher values clear backlogs faster when many events end in sequence, but require GPUs to be available. The typical value is 2.
postprod.jobMaxAttemptsHelp: How many times the pipeline retries a job before marking it as failed. Backoff between attempts grows exponentially (from 30 seconds up to 2 hours). The typical value is 5.
postprod.artifactRetentionDaysHelp: Left at 0, AI artifacts follow the event's own retention schedule. A positive value keeps them for the given number of days even after the event is closed, until a daily cron deletes them. Deletion includes speaker names and job payloads, consistent with docs/GDPR.md.
postprod.consentDisclosureSection: AI notice in the waiting room
postprod.consentDisclosureHelp: Text shown to participants in the waiting room when the event uses AI post-production (transcription, summary, translation, dubbing). Customisable per language. Leave empty to use the built-in default text.
postprod.consentDisclosurePlaceholder: E.g. This event will be automatically transcribed and summarised with AI tools after the live session…

scaling.intro: Parameters that describe the cluster capacity the platform runs on. Defaults are calibrated for Azure F16s_v2 (16 vCPU / 32 GiB) for JVB and F4s_v2 (4 vCPU / 8 GiB) for Jibri. On different hardware tweak these — the scaler recomputes immediately.
scaling.jvbCpuCoresPerPodHelp: CPU cores requested by the JVB container (resources.requests.cpu). Azure F16s_v2 = 16.
scaling.jvbReceiversPerCoreHelp: How many receive-only viewers fit in 1 vCPU. Calibrated to 18.75 (300 webinar ÷ 16 cores) on F16s_v2.
scaling.jvbSendersPerCoreHelp: How many senders (mic+webcam) fit in 1 vCPU. Calibrated to 3.125 (50 all-sender ÷ 16 cores) on F16s_v2.
scaling.jvbMaxReplicasHelp: Hard cap to prevent runaway scale-up. Default 6.
scaling.jibriCpuCoresPerPodHelp: Cores requested by the Jibri container (ffmpeg + headless Chrome). Azure F4s_v2 = 4.
scaling.defaultSenderRatioPctHelp: Share of expected participants that will actually send media (mic+webcam) when the event doesn't specify. Webinar 10-30%, panel 50-100%.
scaling.previewBody: Event with {participants} expected attendees at {ratio}% senders → {senders} senders + {receivers} passive → {cores} estimated cores → {jvbs} JVBs.
scaling.eventGracePeriodMinutesHelp: Extra time granted after the scheduled end before closing the call. 0 = hard stop, -1 = never auto-close (let the idle-cleanup decide), 15 = banner then close after 15 minutes. Each event can override.
scaling.videoQualityHelp: Preset applied to every event (overridable per event). Higher quality needs more bandwidth — “High” is the best trade-off. Takes effect immediately, no restart.

videoQualityDesc.MAX: Full HD 1080p, up to ~4 Mbps, stereo Opus 510 kbps audio. Top fidelity for small high-stakes calls — uses more bandwidth and loads the bridge more.
videoQualityDesc.HIGH: 720p with a capped bitrate (~2.2 Mbps peak) and 96 kbps Opus audio. Great quality without maxing bandwidth: the recommended production trade-off.
videoQualityDesc.BALANCED: 540p with a contained bitrate (~1 Mbps) and fewer simultaneous incoming videos. Good for large audiences or weak networks.
videoQualityDesc.SAVE_DATA: 360p at minimal bitrate (~600 kbps) with unused-layer suspension. For very slow connections.

header.slimBarDescription: The thin bar at the top of the page identifying the parent organization.

features.guestAccessEnabledHelp: Allow guest access to live events
features.publicRegistrationEnabledHelp: Anyone can create events (if disabled, only admins can)
features.calendarPublicHelp: Publish the event calendar. Visitors can see scheduled events.
features.parseTitleKickerHelp: If an event title contains the "|" character, the part before it is rendered as a small uppercase label above the main title. Example: "Coffee chat | Trying the new OSS tool" → label "COFFEE CHAT" + title "Trying the new OSS tool".
features.jvbScalingHelp: Control how long to wait before shutting down an idle video node, and how early to start it for a scheduled event.
features.jvbInactiveGraceMinutesHelp: How long the conference must stay empty before the video node is scaled down. Recommended 45 minutes. Range 5-240.
features.jvbPreScaleMinutesHelp: How many minutes before startsAt to boot the video node for a scheduled event. Recommended 10 minutes.
features.jvbEmptyCloseMinutesHelp: If a LIVE room that already had participants stays empty for this many minutes, it is closed for good (ENDED) — so a moderator-less event closes itself for inactivity, without waiting for its scheduled end. Default 15 (on). -1 = disabled. 0 = close on the first empty poll.
features.waitingRoomLeadMinutesHelp: Registering within this many minutes of the start goes straight to the waiting room; earlier registrations see a confirmation with a calendar link. 0 = always show the confirmation until start. Recommended 15.
features.reactionsModeHelp: Native = Jitsi's own reactions button in the control bar, lightweight and ephemeral (not counted). Custom = the app's reactions bar at bottom-left, with counts and analytics. Default: Native.
features.jvbStressWarnPercentHelp: Above this threshold the scaler adds 1 extra JVB replica even if predictive sizing doesn't require it. Default 50.
features.jvbStressCriticalPercentHelp: Above this threshold the scaler adds 2 extra JVB replicas. Default 70. Must be higher than warn.
features.jvbProvisioningTimeoutMinutesHelp: After how many minutes since startsAt an event without JVB ready is flagged as 'degraded' on the status page. Default 15. Covers node cold-start (~3-5 min) plus safety margin.
features.statusPollIntervalSecondsHelp: How often the /status page refreshes from the browser. Default 30. Lower values = fresher data but more load on the app.
features.emailSenderHelp: How the sender appears in a registrant’s inbox. The sending address stays in the deployment configuration — it has to match what the SMTP server is authorised to send as.
features.emailFromNameHelp: Empty = the name configured in the deployment (SMTP_FROM_NAME) is used. Emails are still sent from the system address.
features.emailReplyToHelp: Emails come from a no-reply address: without this, a reply reaches nobody.
features.gravatarEnabledHelp: Attendees who registered with an email that has a Gravatar will show it in the room. The Gravatar address embeds a hash of the email, is visible to every participant, and each load is a request to a third party: declare it in your privacy notice. When off, the platform’s own initials avatars are used.

instantCall.title: Instant video call

instantCall.passwordHint: If set, guests must type this password to join. Minimum 4 characters. Moderators don't need it.
instantCall.passwordTooShort: Password must be at least 4 characters.
instantCall.maxParticipantsHint: Raise this if you expect a larger audience: the scaler uses this number to decide how many JVB replicas to allocate. Instant calls have no pre-scaling, so for more than ~50 participants prefer a scheduled event with at least 15 minutes' lead time.
instantCall.hint: Everyone can enable microphone, webcam and share screen. Only you can record and end the call.

eventDetail.aiManageNotEnded: Available once the event has ended and the recording has been processed by the AI pipeline.
eventDetail.aiGenerateQueued: AI pipeline started: transcription and summary are queued. Follow progress in post-production management.
eventDetail.aiGenerateAlready: The AI pipeline was already started for this recording: no new jobs added.
eventDetail.aiGenerateError: Could not start the AI pipeline. Check that AI is enabled site-wide and that a recording exists.

eventDetail.aiGenerateNoRecording: No recording: post-event AI needs a recording (capture only happens during the event). You can still manage post-production here.
eventDetail.duplicateNextHint: Creates a draft carrying this event’s entire configuration (recording, separate tracks, AI, agenda, reminders). If the event has a cadence the date moves to the next occurrence; otherwise it is left for you to set.

rubrica.title: Address book
rubrica.subtitle: People who have consented to be added to the address book (GDPR art. 6.1.a). Profile information is updated automatically on every re-registration.

eventAnalytics.title: Statistics
eventAnalytics.notEndedYet: The event has not ended yet: statistics are partial and update in real time.
eventAnalytics.cappedNote: Very large event: some metrics are computed on a sample (row cap reached).
eventAnalytics.scoreProxy: A 0–100 index of how participative the audience was. It is a participation proxy, not a measure of cognitive attention — read it with the event format in mind (lecture vs workshop), never as a quality grade.
eventAnalytics.scoreUnavailable: Not enough data to compute the score.
eventAnalytics.handRaisesSessionsSub: from {n, plural, one {# session} other {# sessions}}
eventAnalytics.timelineEmpty: No interaction recorded during the call.
eventAnalytics.timelinePeak: Interaction peaked at {at} ({n} events in the bucket).
eventAnalytics.audioUnavailable: Audio statistics unavailable — event was not recorded (or the recording had no speech).
eventAnalytics.privacyNote: Per-person statistics are pseudonymous by default. Anonymous aggregates are kept in the event recap; raw rows are deleted when retention expires.

postprod.title: AI post-production pipeline
postprod.subtitle: Transcripts, summaries and translations produced by the in-cluster AI pipeline. Map speakers, re-run or cancel jobs.
postprod.totalRows: {count, plural, =0 {no recordings} one {1 recording} other {# recordings}}
postprod.rerunTooltip: Creates a new run by bumping runCount; previous artifacts persist until retention
postprod.cancelConfirm: Cancel all pending jobs for this recording?
postprod.loadError: Could not load the job list
postprod.empty: No recordings match the current filters
postprod.suggestedTitle: Name suggested by the AI pipeline based on proper nouns mentioned in the transcript. Click to fill the field (you can still edit it before saving).
postprod.editHint: Fix the text or reassign the speaker of each segment. On save, the transcript and the source-language subtitles are updated. Translations and dubbing are NOT regenerated: use “Re-run” if you also need the other languages refreshed.
postprod.editNotReady: No transcript is available for this recording yet. Start (or re-run) AI processing from the controls above: transcription is the first step, then you can correct it here and generate the summary and translations.
postprod.editProcessing: AI processing in progress: the transcript will appear here when it finishes. Refresh the page in a few minutes.
postprod.editSessionExpired: Admin session expired. Reload the page and sign in again to edit the transcript.
postprod.editDirty: {n, plural, one {1 unsaved change} other {# unsaved changes}}
postprod.editMediaError: Could not load the recording's audio: playback is unavailable. You can still correct the text and speakers of each segment.
postprod.summaryEmptyProcessing: The AI summary isn't ready yet: processing is in progress. Refresh the page in a few minutes.
postprod.summaryEmptyFailed: Summary generation failed. Check the pipeline status and re-run processing from the controls above.
postprod.summaryEmptyNone: No AI summary has been generated for this recording. Start processing from the controls above.
postprod.editSeek: Jump to this point in the video
postprod.archivePiiNote: Isolated per-participant audio tracks are sensitive personal data (isolated voice): this section is restricted to admins/moderators and is never publicly exposed.
postprod.archiveNoTracks: No per-participant tracks available for this recording (multi-track recording not enabled, or tracks already deleted for GDPR minimization).
postprod.archiveAudioHint: Use the player's "Audio" selector to listen to the mix or a single participant's isolated track, synced to the video.
postprod.archiveIntro: Generate an MKV file with the video, one audio track per participant (labelled with their name, precisely aligned) and subtitles. Meant for download and archival: browsers can't switch audio tracks inside an MKV, so use the player above for playback.

tm.loadError: Could not load the translation languages.

ov.intro: What the AI pipeline produced for this recording, language by language.

rel.title: AI reliability

rel.transcriptNotProduced: Transcript not produced yet (pipeline not run).
rel.transcriptUnavailable: Transcript present but not analysable here (stored in blob storage).

warn.transcriptEmpty: Empty transcript: the source audio appears silent or without speech. The derived summary, translations and dubbing are NOT trustworthy.
warn.lowConfidence: Low transcription confidence: review the text before publishing.
warn.manyLowConfSegments: Many low-confidence segments: the transcript may contain errors.
warn.highNoSpeech: Audio is largely silent or without speech: the transcript may be hallucinated.

postprod.archiveSourceHint: Original event video. Separate per-participant tracks and the MKV archive require multi-track recording (not enabled for this event).
postprod.manageRerunNoop: No job started: AI is disabled for this event, or the pipeline is paused. Use “Generate AI”.

postprod.generateAiConfirm: Run the AI pipeline (transcription and summary) on this recording. AI processing will be enabled for the event and starts in the background. Translations are added afterwards, one language at a time, from the “Translations” tab.

howItWorks.title: How to take part
howItWorks.subtitle: Just a few steps, with no account and no installs.

step1.title: Register
step1.desc: Sign up to the event for free from its public page.

step2.title: Get the link
step2.desc: You receive an email with your personal link and a calendar attachment.

step3.title: Join
step3.desc: Enter the event from your browser, with nothing to install.

step4.title: Interact
step4.desc: Ask questions, vote, answer polls and join the discussion.

during.title: What you can do during an event
during.subtitle: Not just watch: events are interactive.

qa.title: Questions and answers
qa.desc: Ask questions and upvote others': the moderator answers live.

polls.title: Polls
polls.desc: Answer polls in real time and see the results instantly.

wordcloud.title: Word cloud
wordcloud.desc: Add your words to the event's shared word cloud.

reactions.title: Reactions and raised hands
reactions.desc: React and ask to speak by raising your hand: the queue is handled in order.

chat.title: Chat
chat.desc: Write in the event chat and follow the conversation with other participants.

agenda.title: Notes and checklist
agenda.desc: Follow the notes and checklist the moderators share during the event.

projectTeaser.title: Open source and sovereign, built by government for government
projectTeaser.body: PA Webinar is the Department for Digital Transformation's platform for public digital events: the .italia design system, the Jitsi Meet video engine and a fully in-cluster AI pipeline. It is released as open source so any administration can reuse it.

sovereignty.title: Data sovereignty
sovereignty.desc: Self-hosted on the administration's infrastructure: data never leaves and there is no CLOUD Act exposure.

openSource.title: Open source EUPL-1.2
openSource.desc: Code reusable by any administration, with a public SBOM and service inventory.

gdpr.title: GDPR-by-design
gdpr.desc: PII encrypted at rest, granular consents and configurable retention.

afterEvent.title: After the event
afterEvent.subtitle: Nothing is lost: everything stays available and accessible.

library.title: Recordings library
library.desc: Rewatch past events in the public library, with the archive of questions and polls.

transcript.title: Transcript and subtitles
transcript.desc: Each recording can have a transcript and subtitles, generated automatically by the in-cluster AI pipeline.

translation.title: Automatic translation
translation.desc: Subtitles and summary translated into several languages, so you can follow the event in another language too.

dubbing.title: Audio dubbing
dubbing.desc: A dubbed audio track with a neutral synthetic voice (no voice cloning), to listen to the event in another language.

polls.title: Polls
polls.noPolls: No polls yet. The moderator will create them during the event.

materials.title: Materials

consent.multitrackRequired: Consent to recording your audio track is required to participate in this event
consent.futureCommunications: I would like to receive information about future events organized by DTD

consent.addressBookHelp: Separate consent (GDPR art. 6.1.a). You can withdraw it at any time using the "Remove me from the address book" link in emails. Your name and organization are taken from your registration profile.

privacy.title: Privacy policy
privacy.intro: This page summarises the processing of personal data related to the use of the PA Webinar platform and participation in hosted public events.

controller.title: Data controller
controller.body: Personal data is processed within the institutional activities of the Department for Digital Transformation to support the technical and organisational management of public online events.

data.title: Processed data and purposes
data.body: The platform processes only the data strictly necessary for registration and participation in events, such as name, email address and any optional fields configured by the organiser. Data is used to send the personal access link, manage attendance, deliver reminders when applicable and support service security obligations.

retention.title: Retention and rights
retention.body: Data is retained for the period configured for each event and then automatically deleted, unless different legal obligations apply. Data subjects may exercise their GDPR rights at any time, including access, rectification and erasure, through the Department's institutional channels.

automatedProcessing.title: AI-generated transcript, summary and dubbing
automatedProcessing.body: For events that opt in, after the live session ends the recording is processed by an artificial-intelligence pipeline running entirely on the Department's own cluster infrastructure. The pipeline produces the transcript of the speech (with speaker separation), a plain-language summary, optional translations into other European languages and a dubbed version of the audio with a generic synthetic voice. The models used (WhisperX for transcription, Mistral-Small for summary and translation, Piper TTS for dubbing) all run on dedicated GPUs inside the cluster: no participant data is sent to third-party services. The voice of the participants is not cloned and is not stored as biometric data (Art. 9 GDPR): the dubbing uses pre-trained public synthetic voices, assigned to the different speakers in an arbitrary way. Each recording exposes the exact snapshot of the models used on the event page ('AI processing transparency'). Generated content may contain inaccuracies: the authoritative reference is always the original video (AI Act, Art. 50).

note.title: Request your data
note.body: If you joined an event and want to verify which personal data is associated with your email address, you can use the dedicated feature available on the platform.

accessibility.title: Accessibility statement
accessibility.intro: The Department for Digital Transformation is committed to making PA Webinar accessible, in line with Italian Law 4/2004, EU Directive 2016/2102 and AgID's guidelines on the accessibility of ICT tools.

conformance.title: Conformance status
conformance.body: PA Webinar is designed to conform to WCAG 2.1 level AA. Each administration providing the service must publish its own statement, indicating the actual conformance status (fully, partially or non conformant) based on an up-to-date assessment.

nonAccessible.title: Non-accessible content
nonAccessible.body: The video room is provided through Jitsi Meet: some real-time features may have limitations with assistive technologies. Content generated automatically by AI (transcripts, subtitles, translations and dubbing) may contain inaccuracies and is always flagged as such.

preparation.title: Preparation of this statement
preparation.body: This template statement was prepared on the basis of a self-assessment carried out by the service provider. The interface is built with the .italia design system, with attention to keyboard navigation, content semantics, colour contrast and screen-reader compatibility. The preparation and last-review dates must be filled in at publication time.

feedback.title: Feedback and contact
feedback.body: To report cases of non-conformance, or to request information and content excluded from the scope of the regulation, you can use the institutional contact channels of the administration providing the service, indicating the page concerned and a description of the problem.

enforcement.title: Enforcement procedure
enforcement.body: If the response is unsatisfactory or no reply is received within 30 days, you can contact AgID's Digital Civic Ombudsman (Difensore civico per il digitale).

note.title: Reuse and publication of the statement
note.body: PA Webinar is open-source software reusable by other administrations. Each body adopting the platform must prepare and publish its own accessibility statement through AgID's official form, replacing this template text with its own data.

legalNotes.title: Legal notes
legalNotes.intro: Access to and use of the PA Webinar platform is subject to these notes, which summarise the general terms of use and the main information about the service.

ownership.title: Service ownership
ownership.body: The platform is promoted by the Department for Digital Transformation and made available for the organisation and participation in institutional public online events.

usage.title: Use of the service
usage.body: Users must use the service correctly and must not compromise platform security, feature availability or the proper running of events. Abuse or improper use may lead to restrictions on access to the service.

liability.title: Limitation of liability
liability.body: While appropriate technical and organisational measures are adopted, the Department cannot guarantee the complete absence of interruptions or malfunctions caused by unforeseen events, maintenance activities or external factors. Legal responsibilities remain governed by applicable law.

security.title: Security and transparency
security.intro: The source code of this platform is public and subject to automated security controls. This page summarises the transparency tools available to anyone using the service.

openSource.title: Open source software
openSource.body: The entire platform is released as free software. The source code can be inspected, downloaded and audited by anyone, in line with the transparency principles of the Italian Public Administration.

sbom.title: SBOM (Software Bill of Materials)
sbom.body: Every release published on GitHub includes an SPDX SBOM for the container image and a CycloneDX SBOM for the application npm dependencies. The files are attached directly to the release and can be used for security and compliance audits.

scorecard.title: OpenSSF Scorecard
scorecard.body: The repository is analysed periodically by OpenSSF Scorecard, which automatically evaluates dozens of project security practices (branch protection, least-privilege workflows, signed commits, dependency management). The current score is public.

scanning.title: Automated scanning
scanning.body: Dependencies are continuously monitored via Dependabot and npm audit. The release pipeline produces traceable images through the org.opencontainers.image.revision label, binding each image to the exact commit it was built from.

disclosure.title: Vulnerability disclosure
disclosure.body: The responsible disclosure policy is described in the SECURITY.md file of the repository. Security issues can be reported confidentially following the instructions provided there.

serviceInventory.title: Provider service inventory
serviceInventory.intro: For this instance, the document below lists both the software dependencies (DEV) and the operational services (OPS) that make up the platform. It is published in CycloneDX 1.6, an OWASP open standard that describes code libraries and third-party managed services in a single document.
serviceInventory.notPublishedBody: The operator of this instance has not yet published a service inventory. The SERVICE_INVENTORY_URL configuration variable has not been set for this deployment.
serviceInventory.fetchErrorBody: The inventory JSON file is currently unreachable. Please try again later or contact the service owner.

sections.componentsIntro: Libraries, containers and OS packages that form the application. Tied to the code version; reproducible by anyone with access to the source.
sections.servicesIntro: Third-party managed services this instance depends on. Tenant-scoped information that changes whenever the operator reconfigures the deployment or switches provider.

stack.title: Architecture at a glance
stack.intro: A high-level view of the four layers that make up the platform: from user access down to the runtime platform.

access.title: Access
access.sub: Web entry point and TLS termination

stack.note: The complete list of components and services, with versions, hashes and technical properties, is available in the sections above and in the downloadable JSON.

status.title: System status
status.subtitle: Real-time overview of platform service status.
status.jvbStandbyDetail: Scale-to-zero active — no upcoming events. JVB nodes will be created automatically before an event starts.
status.jvbOctoActive: Octo active: {bridges} bridges, {relayMbps} Mbps inter-bridge relay
status.staleJvbDetail: At least one active event has been waiting longer than the configured timeout and the video bridge isn't available yet. Check the cluster autoscaler or JVB pod logs.
status.staleJibriDetail: At least one event with recording enabled has been waiting longer than the configured timeout and Jibri isn't available yet.

postprod.title: Post-event pipeline
postprod.subtitle: Transcription, summary, translation and dubbing of the recording
postprod.whatItDoes: Once an event has ended the recording goes through an internal pipeline: WhisperX produces the transcript, a Mistral model handles the summary and translations, Piper TTS generates the dubbed audio. All the models run on a cluster GPU that stays on only for the duration of the work, so energy use follows actual demand.
postprod.aiBadge: The transcript, summary and dubbing for this event are generated by AI models and may contain inaccuracies. Always refer back to the original video when you need full certainty.
postprod.idleMessage: Pipeline ready, no recordings to process right now.
postprod.artifactsProduced: {count, plural, =0 {no artifacts} one {1 artifact produced} other {# artifacts produced}}
postprod.eventsEnabled: {count, plural, =0 {no AI-enabled events} one {1 event with AI active} other {# events with AI active}}

infraMap.title: Infrastructure map
infraMap.subtitle: Real-time visualization of the architecture and service status

descriptions.app: The website where you access events
descriptions.database: Where events and registrations are stored
descriptions.redis: Real-time chat message exchange between servers
descriptions.jicofo: Coordinates who participates in each conference
descriptions.jvb: Transmits audio and video to participants
descriptions.postprod: Transcribes, summarises and translates recordings using in-cluster AI models (NVIDIA A100 GPU). Activates only when an event opts into AI features.

app.healthy: The web portal is working correctly and responding to requests.

database.degraded: The database is responding, but slower than usual. Operations may take longer.
database.down: The database is not responding. The platform cannot function properly.

redis.healthy: Real-time chat service is responding correctly.
redis.degraded: Real-time chat service is slow: messages may reach other servers with delay.
redis.down: Real-time chat service is unreachable. Live chat cannot fan out messages between servers — messages are still persisted to the database but users don't see each other in real time.

jitsiWeb.healthy: The video conference room is reachable and ready for events.
jitsiWeb.down: The video conference room is not reachable. It is not possible to start or join events.
jitsiWeb.standby: The video conference room is not configured.

prosody.healthy: Internal messaging between video services is working correctly.
prosody.down: Internal messaging is not reachable. Video services cannot communicate with each other.
prosody.standby: The messaging service is not configured.

jicofo.healthy: The conference coordinator is active and managing sessions.
jicofo.down: The conference coordinator is not reachable. It is not possible to create new conferences.
jicofo.standby: The conference coordinator is not configured.

jvb.healthy: The video bridge is active and transmitting audio/video to participants.
jvb.scaling: The video bridge is preparing for an upcoming event. Servers are being created automatically.
jvb.standby: The video bridge is on standby (scale-to-zero). No event currently needs the bridge — it will activate automatically before a scheduled event or on the first user join.
jvb.stale: An event has been waiting for the video bridge longer than the configured timeout. Check the cluster autoscaler and JVB pod logs.

jibri.healthy: The recording service is ready and available.
jibri.busy: The recording service is active and recording a session in progress.
jibri.down: The recording service is not reachable. Events cannot be recorded.
jibri.standby: Jibri is on standby (scale-to-zero). Storage is configured but no active event requires recording — it will activate automatically only when a LIVE event has recording enabled.
jibri.unconfigured: Video recording is not configured (no storage backend). Events will not be recorded.
jibri.stale: An event with recording enabled has been waiting for Jibri longer than the configured timeout. Check Jibri pod logs.

smtp.healthy: The email service is configured and ready to send notifications.
smtp.standby: The email service is not configured. Confirmation emails and reminders will not be sent.

postprod.healthy: The AI pipeline is processing a recording (GPU worker active).
postprod.scaling: Postprod jobs queued — the GPU node is starting up (cold-start ~3 min).
postprod.standby: AI pipeline enabled but idle: no recordings to process. The GPU node is powered down to save energy.
postprod.degraded: One or more AI jobs failed in the last 24h. Check /admin/postprod for details.

impacts.database: The platform cannot load events, manage registrations, or save data.
impacts.redis: Live chat messages don't fan out across servers. Users only see messages sent on their own pod.
impacts.jitsiWeb: Participants cannot access the video conference room.
impacts.prosody: Video services cannot communicate. Conferences will not work.
impacts.jicofo: It is not possible to create or manage new video conferences.
impacts.jvbScaling: The video bridge is activating. The event will be able to start shortly.
impacts.jvbStale: An event has been waiting for the bridge longer than the configured timeout: participants cannot join.
impacts.jibri: Events cannot be recorded. Participants can still join and participate.
impacts.jibriStale: An event with recording enabled has been waiting for Jibri past the timeout: the session is not being recorded.
impacts.smtp: Registration confirmation emails and reminders will not be sent.
impacts.postprodDegraded: Transcripts and summaries for some recent recordings were not generated. Video recordings remain available.

overallVerdicts.operational: All services are working correctly. The platform is fully operational.
overallVerdicts.degraded: Some services are experiencing slowdowns. The platform is operational but performance may be reduced.
overallVerdicts.outage: One or more services are unavailable. Some platform features may not work.

infraMap.verdictSectionSubtitle: Details on services that require attention

footer.presidencyName: Presidency of the Council of Ministers

changelog.title: What's new & changelog
changelog.intro: Every version of the platform, newest first. Each entry summarizes the main new features, fixes and improvements.
changelog.sourceNote: Notes are curated from the project's tags, pull requests and commits.

video.audioSyntheticBanner: AI-dubbed audio — synthetic voice, automatically generated content.

postprod.empty: No transcript available for this event.
postprod.aiBadgeBody: Transcript and summary are produced automatically by the in-cluster AI pipeline. They may contain errors; the video remains the authoritative source.

hero.title: Summary
hero.topicsLabel: Chapters — click to jump to that point in the video

postprod.bookmarksEmpty: You haven't saved any moments yet. Open a transcript segment and click the star to add it here.
postprod.miniPlayerHint: Stay close to the video while you scroll
postprod.lowConfidenceTitle: This part of the transcript has low confidence: the recording may contain different words. Always refer back to the original video.
postprod.dubAvailableHint: Dubbed audio available in {langs}. Use the audio selector in the player controls to listen to the translated version.

provenance.intro: These are the models that produced the transcript, summary and dubbing for this recording. The snapshot is saved when the processing finishes: you see what was actually used at that time, regardless of any later changes to the settings.

llm.title: Summary and translation
llm.hint: Meeting summary and translation of transcript and summary.

tts.title: Dubbing
tts.hint: Speech synthesis for the audio in the target languages.

languages.title: Languages
languages.hint: Source of the recording and translation/dubbing targets.

postprod.overlapTitle: Simultaneous speech: more than one person is speaking at the same time in this interval.

waiting.startingSoon: The event is about to start, please wait for the organizer…
waiting.connectedParticipants: {count, plural, =0 {No participants} =1 {1 participant connected} other {{count} participants connected}}
waiting.enterLiveDesc: Join live. You can always access the recording after the event.
waiting.watchFromStartDesc: Watch the recording from the start and join live whenever you want.
waiting.recordingAvailableAfter: The recording will be available after the event.
waiting.autoRefreshHint: The page will automatically refresh when the event starts.
waiting.peopleWaiting: {count, plural, =0 {} =1 {1 person waiting} other {{count} people waiting}}
waiting.jvbScalingDetail: Video servers are starting up. This takes about 2 minutes.

warmup.almostReadyDetail: A few more moments and the join button will enable itself.
warmup.honestHint: Typical wait: under a minute when the infrastructure is warm, 3–5 minutes on a cold start. Meanwhile you can check your camera and microphone or use the chat.

waiting.warmingUpDetail: We're spinning up the video servers. Get yourself ready in the meantime — everything will be live in 2-3 minutes.
waiting.netiquetteBullet1: Arrive a few minutes early to test your microphone and camera
waiting.netiquetteBullet2: Turn camera and mic on only when speaking — raise your hand to request the floor
waiting.netiquetteBullet3: Use chat for quick exchanges, Q&A for questions to the speakers
waiting.netiquetteBullet4: Recording, when enabled, is controlled by moderators and marked with a red indicator
waiting.watchCatchupDesc: Watch the partial recording and catch up with the live stream when you want
waiting.chatPreviewHint: Read what other participants are saying while you get ready
waiting.startEventError: Error starting the event. Please try again.
waiting.emailInvalid: Please enter a valid email address
waiting.chatLockedMessage: Enter your name to join the event chat.
waiting.aiNotice: After it ends, the recording will be processed by AI tools to generate a transcript, summary and any translations and dubbing. The artifacts may contain inaccuracies; the video remains the authoritative source.
waiting.multitrackConsentRequired: You must accept the recording of your audio track to enter

recording.publishDesc: Make the video accessible on the event page
recording.retentionNote: The video will still follow the event's GDPR retention
recording.deleteConfirm: Are you sure? The video will be permanently deleted.
recording.notConfigured: Jibri is not configured. Video recording is not available.
recording.disabledHint: Video recording is not enabled for this event. To enable it open the Settings section above and toggle "Recording" on.

postEvent.pageVisibleHelp: When off (or once the visibility window expires), the concluded event's public page returns 404 and the event drops out of listings and the sitemap.
postEvent.libraryListedHelp: Show this event in the public library (/video-library). Requires a published recording or a YouTube video, and the event page to be visible.
postEvent.libraryListedNoRecording: No published recording: the event won't appear in the library until you publish the recording or add a YouTube video.
postEvent.emailEnabled: Send recap email when the event ends
postEvent.emailEnabledHelp: On conclusion, sends registered participants a thank-you email with links to the recap and feedback, and the moderator a short recap. Sent only once.
postEvent.feedbackEnabled: Feedback survey active at event end
postEvent.dataRetentionNote: Participants' personal data will be deleted according to event retention ({days} days). Event content will remain available while the page is visible.
postEvent.availableUntil: This event and its content will be available until {date}.

provisioning.descriptionFromIdle: The room had been paused due to inactivity to save energy. We're restarting it now — this usually takes 2-3 minutes.
provisioning.descriptionColdStart: We're setting up the virtual room for this event. It takes about 2-3 minutes: you'll be redirected automatically when everything is ready.
provisioning.tempError: Retrying to reach the server, no action needed.
provisioning.scaleToZeroNote: To reduce environmental impact, video nodes are powered off when nobody is in a call. They restart automatically on the first join.

optOut.title: Remove me from the DTD address book
optOut.intro: You are about to withdraw your consent to be listed in the DTD address book. After confirmation you will no longer be contacted for invitations to similar events.
optOut.missingToken: The link is invalid or incomplete. Please check the original email.
optOut.invalidToken: The link is expired or invalid. Contact the event organizer for help.
optOut.notFound: No person matches this link — they may already have been removed.
optOut.previewHeader: You are about to remove from the address book:
optOut.warning: This does not delete historical event registrations.
optOut.successBody: Your data is no longer in the DTD address book.

errors.generic: Something went wrong. Please try again later.

deviceCheck.permissionNeeded: Allow camera and microphone to test them. You can also join without.

garden.pickerHelp: This character represents you in the waiting garden. You can change it any time.
